title: 2 Indians killed in Oman, 10 injured after Iranian drone strikes in Oman
content: Two Indian nationals have died and 10 others were injured in an attack in Sohar city, Oman. Five of the injured have been discharged from the hospital, while the remaining five are receiving medical treatment. Indian authorities are in contact with Omani officials and are providing assistance to the affected nationals.

title: 'About to surrender': Trump makes big claim on Iran amid ongoing conflict
content: US President Trump claimed that Iran is "about to surrender" during a G7 call earlier this week, while describing Iran regime as removing a "cancer." Amidst escalating regional strikes, Iran reportedly targeted the USS Abraham Lincoln, though the US denied the incident. CENTCOM confirmed the carrier strike group's ongoing support for operations.

title: Why did market crash? Nifty50 closes at 23,151, Sensex down over 1,400 points
content: Stock market crash today: Continuing the down trend, Nifty50 and BSE Sensex tanked in trade on Friday as oil prices climbed and the Strait of Hormuz closure halted important global trade. While Nifty50 went below 23,200, BSE Sensex was down over 1,400 points.

title: Palantir CEO Alex Karp admits using Anthropic's Claude after Pentagon ban
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp confirmed continued use of Anthropic's Claude AI models despite the Pentagon labeling the company a supply chain risk.  The Department of Defense is planning a phase-out, but acknowledges a transition will take time, with potential exemptions for mission-critical operations. This highlights the challenges in disentangling deeply embedded AI systems from defense operations.

title: 'CSK is incomplete without him': Pathan drops big statement on Dhoni's IPL future
content: Former India all-rounder Irfan Pathan believes this IPL season could be MS Dhoni's last for Chennai Super Kings.  Pathan emphasized Dhoni's crucial role in team unity and grooming future leaders, even if his playing time is limited.  He suggested CSK aims for a title win to give the legend a fitting farewell.

title: Middle East crisis: 4 crew members killed after US refueling aircraft crashes in Iraq
content: A US KC-135 refueling aircraft crashed in western Iraq on Friday, resulting in the deaths of four of its six crew members. The US military confirmed the incident is under investigation but ruled out hostile or friendly fire. This marks the fourth US military aircraft loss in the Middle East conflict.

title: Thousands of Chinese boats form unusual patterns near Taiwan -what it could mean
content: Thousands of Chinese fishing boats formed unusual geometric patterns in the East China Sea, raising questions about potential military or strategic mobilization. Experts suggest these large-scale, coordinated gatherings, unlike normal fishing, may indicate preparations for crisis scenarios involving Taiwan or Japan.

title: ‘Border 2’ OTT release: When and where to stream the war drama
content: Sunny Deol’s ‘Border 2’ (Jan 23, 2026 release) earned Rs 392cr+ worldwide. Now on OTT from March 20. Anurag Singh directs 1971 Indo-Pak war saga with Varun Dhawan, Diljit Dosanjh, Ahan Shetty honoring real heroes. Action-packed drama blends battles, sacrifices, and hit track “Ghar Kab Aoge.”

title: LPG shortage: What Infosys and HCL Tech's advisory to employees says
content: IT giants Infosys and HCLTech are implementing temporary measures due to an LPG gas shortage impacting India. Infosys has adjusted food court menus and cooking methods across its Bengaluru, Pune, and Chennai campuses. HCLTech has advised its Chennai employees to work from home as cafeteria vendors face operational challenges.

title: The Hundred: 'We had a few players in our list,' says Badani on PAK players
content: Southern Braves' head coach Hemang Badani emphasised sticking to their auction plan, prioritising team balance over external narratives as debate rages over Pakistan players' involvement in The Hundred. While Pakistani players were considered, the franchise secured key overseas and local talent like Adil Rashid and Nikhil Chaudhary, focusing on acquiring the best available skill sets for their squad.

title: How much 18K, 22K and 24K gold costs in your city? Check prices
content: Gold prices saw a significant dip on Friday, with futures trade closing at Rs 1.59 lakh per 10 grams. This decline was influenced by weaker international trends and a stronger US dollar. Analysts highlight the strengthening dollar as a key pressure point, while geopolitical risks and economic signals remain under close observation by traders.

title: $3.5 a shot vs $3m Patriot: How high-energy lasers could replace missiles against Iranian attacks
content: High-energy lasers are emerging as a cost-effective defense against drones and missiles, with costs as low as $3.50 per shot compared to millions for missile interceptors. Despite decades of development and some deployments, challenges remain in scaling the technology for widespread military use due to environmental limitations and production hurdles.

title: Middle East crisis: Jaishankar holds fourth call with Iranian FM; bilateral ties in focus
content: India's External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar held his fourth phone call with Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi, discussing bilateral ties and BRICS issues amidst Middle East tensions. The conversation followed recent discussions on the Strait of Hormuz and maritime security, with Iran blaming US actions for regional instability.

title: Hormuz miscalculation? Trump sideswiped by Iran's closure of Strait
content: Washington underestimated Iran's threat to close the Strait of Hormuz, causing global oil market jitters. Despite planning, economic fallout wasn't fully accounted for, leaving the US scrambling. While officials claim preparedness, limited options and high risks for naval escorts are evident. Iran signals the strait's closure remains a pressure tool.

title: Former Chelsea and Arsenal star Amy Carr dies at 35 after long battle with brain tumour
content: Former England youth international Amy Carr has sadly passed away at the age of 35 after fighting a brain tumour for more than a decade. Carr, who once played for top clubs such as Chelsea F.C. Women, Arsenal W.F.C., and Reading F.C. Women, inspired many people through her courage and determination during her illness. She was first diagnosed with a high-grade brain tumour in 2015.

title: Afghanistan hits Pakistan's Kohat military fort in retaliatory drone strikes
content: Afghanistan retaliated against Pakistani strikes that killed four in Kabul and Kandahar. Afghan forces claimed to have targeted Pakistani military centers in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, including a fortress and command center, following alleged "night-time violations" along the disputed Durand Line. This escalation intensifies existing tensions between the two nations.

title: How LPG issue, oil price surge & Hormuz crisis may hit Indian industry: Explained in 10 points
content: The scarcity of commercial LPG has emerged after shipments from the Gulf region were disrupted amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia. India relies heavily on imports from this region for its LPG requirements.

title: Who is Vanshika Chadha? Meet Kuldeep Yadav’s bride-to-be
content: Indian cricketer Kuldeep Yadav will marry his longtime partner Vanshika Chadha on March 14 in Mussoorie. The couple, childhood friends from Kanpur, saw their friendship grow into a lifelong relationship. Vanshika works at the Life Insurance Corporation of India and maintains a private life away from the spotlight. Wedding festivities begin March 13, with cricket stars like Virat Kohli expected to attend.

title: If Iran pull out of FIFA World Cup, Here's why Iraq and UAE could replace them
content: Iran's participation in the upcoming FIFA World Cup is uncertain due to geopolitical tensions with the United States, a co-host. Citing security and political concerns, Iranian authorities are contemplating withdrawal. If Iran pulls out, FIFA will need to select a replacement, likely from the Asian Football Confederation, with UAE and Iraq being potential candidates.

title: 'Nobody will assign them responsibilities': Why Supreme Court rejected plea on menstrual leave
content: The Supreme Court declined to mandate menstrual leave for women students and employees nationwide, citing concerns about potential negative impacts on women's employment and the risk of reinforcing gender stereotypes. The court emphasized that policy decisions of this nature should be made by government policymakers after consulting stakeholders, rather than through judicial intervention.

title: From cheering low gas cost to taking pride 'when oil prices go up': Trump’s evolving stance
content: President Trump's stance on rising oil prices has shifted, now framing them as beneficial for the US as a major oil producer. This change follows strikes against Iran, which triggered a surge in global oil and petrol prices, impacting the Strait of Hormuz. The administration is grappling with economic consequences and political considerations ahead of midterm elections.

title: This tiny village in Maharashtra has a magical secret that grows inside pumpkins
content: Miraj, a historic city in Maharashtra, is renowned for its musical instrument making. Craftsmen create exquisite sitars and tanpuras using a special type of pumpkin. This tradition, passed down through generations, faces threats from climate change impacting pumpkin size. The craft requires government support for survival. The disappearance of this art would mean losing music, history, and ancestral voices.

title: Kuldeep Yadav wedding: Chahal arrives in Dehradun, says ‘I will dance a lot’
content: Yuzvendra Chahal arrived in Dehradun for Kuldeep Yadav's wedding in Mussoorie, expressing excitement for the festivities. The spinner is set to tie the knot with fiancée Vanshika on March 14 at the Welcomhotel The Savoy Mussoorie. The event is anticipated to be a private yet star-studded affair with several prominent cricketers expected to attend.

title: Tharoor lauds PM Modi's outreach to Iran president, holding talks
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor praised Prime Minister Modi and EAM Jaishankar for engaging with Iran amidst escalating West Asian tensions. He urged a collective push for peace, highlighting the risks to Indian citizens in the Gulf and the nation's role as a constructive voice.

title: Eight weeks of bloodbath: Is Nifty IT facing a pause or the start of a bigger tide?
content: India's IT stocks are facing an eighth consecutive week of losses, wiping out significant market value and sparking debate. While some fear AI's disruptive impact, others see a buying opportunity amid historical rebounds. Foreign investors have exited, but a domestic fund is increasing its IT holdings, betting on a recovery.

title: 'Vital partner in keeping oil prices stable': US envoy lauds India amid Strait of Hormuz crisis
content: US envoy Sergio Gor voiced grave concerns over escalating tensions at the Strait of Hormuz, warning of potential global oil supply disruptions. He highlighted Iran's actions targeting commercial vessels, including those with Indian casualties, and stressed India's crucial role in maintaining stable oil prices.

title: Agra woman ends life, blames UP cop for false marriage promise in videos
content: A UP Police constable, JB Gautam, was arrested for allegedly abetting the suicide of a woman who accused him of mental harassment and breaking a marriage promise. The woman, who was living separately from her husband, reportedly met Gautam four years ago, and their relationship allegedly led to her distress after he refused to marry her.

title: Hundred top buy Coles' Rs 4.8 cr price tag won't even make IPL auction top 10
content: The Hundred 2026 auction saw significant spending, with James Coles topping the charts at Rs 4.8 crore. However, the IPL 2026 auction dwarfed these figures, with Cameron Green fetching a staggering Rs 25.20 crore.

title: 'These are plans to call women inferior': CJI led-SC bench rejects plea on menstrual leave
content: The Supreme Court has declined to mandate nationwide menstrual leave for women, citing concerns that it could reinforce gender stereotypes and harm employment prospects. While acknowledging voluntary policies are welcome, the court directed authorities to consider the petitioner's representation on the matter, emphasizing potential negative career impacts if made compulsory.

title: US launches 'forced labour' probe against India and 59 other countries: What is Section 301?
content: The US trade representative's office has launched Section 301 investigations into 60 economies, including India, for failing to address forced labor in imports. This move follows a Supreme Court ruling and aims to determine if foreign governments are sufficiently prohibiting goods made with forced labor, impacting US workers and businesses. New tariffs on several countries are possible by summer.

title: US-Iran war: How Strait of Hormuz closure impacts global oil supply - explained in charts
content: The Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy route, is effectively blocked due to the escalating US-Israeli war on Iran, halting tankers traffic and forcing oil producers to cut the oil output. Damaged infrastructure and blocked shipping lanes are causing global supply shocks, leading to surging energy prices and economic strain worldwide.

title: Income cannot be sole decider of OBC creamy layer: SC
content: The Supreme Court ruled that parental job status, not just income, must determine OBC 'creamy layer' exclusion. This decision impacts wards of PSU and private sector employees, aligning their criteria with government employees. The court emphasized that income alone is insufficient for exclusion, stressing the importance of job category and status.

title: Trump tariff ‘collapse’ triggers US trade probe — what it means for India
content: The economies included in the investigation are China, the European Union, Singapore, Switzerland, Norway, Indonesia, Malaysia, Cambodia, Thailand, South Korea, Vietnam, Taiwan, Bangladesh, Mexico, Japan and India.

title: Why Sunrisers Leeds bought Pakistan spinner at The Hundred auction: Vettori explains
content: Sunrisers Leeds' acquisition of Pakistani spinner Abrar Ahmed at The Hundred auction sparked controversy in India. Head coach Daniel Vettori clarified the move was a purely cricketing decision after missing out on Adil Rashid. Vettori emphasised that no internal discussions about avoiding Pakistani players occurred, focusing solely on the best available talent for the team's strategy.

title: Mumbai’s beloved samosa and vada pav disappear from menus as LPG supply tightens
content: Mumbai's beloved street food is facing an unprecedented crisis due to a severe shortage of commercial LPG cylinders. Popular eateries are struggling to operate, with some forced to close or drastically reduce their menus. Vendors are resorting to risky black market purchases to keep their businesses afloat, impacting daily staples for many Mumbaikars.

title: Crude above $100: How countries are racing to streamline oil supplies
content: Global oil markets are reeling from Middle East tensions, with the Strait of Hormuz facing disruptions.  Nations like India are securing Russian crude, while the US and other IEA members release emergency reserves. China eyes its reserves and Russia, as countries worldwide implement measures to stabilize prices and ensure supply.

title: Pocso case against sarpanch’s husband for sexual assault of minor in Telangana
content: A 27-year-old man, husband of a local sarpanch, has been booked under the POCSO Act for allegedly sexually assaulting a 17-year-old girl. The incident occurred on February 23 while the girl's daily wager parents were at work. The accused reportedly entered the house under the guise of counselling the minor, who had dropped out of college.

title: India right in condemning Iran’s aggression, but why silent over Khamenei’s assassination: Congress asks govt.
content: In a social media post on X, Congress communications chief Jairam Ramesh said Mr. Modi as well as External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar had not condemned the U.S.-Israel action

title: Kerala Assembly polls: KPCC tries to persuade K. Sudhakaran to step back from Kannur race; MP’s emotional post sparks debate
content: Sudhakaran’s emotional social media post defending his decision to contest from Kannur triggers political discussion within Congress

title: ‘Love Mocktail 3’ will silence the critics of the franchise’
content: Darling Krishna and Milana Nagaraj, the makers behind the Kannada relationship drama ‘Love Mocktail 3’, open up on the movie.

title: Bill to provide precise definition of ‘transgender’ introduced in Lok Sabha
content: In amendments to the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019, the government has proposed to do away with the principle that recognises transgender people’s right to a “self-perceived gender identity”, and has introduced a definition of “transgender persons”

title: Parents of Bekal Beach Fest train-accident victim found dead
content: Preliminary investigation hints at suicide. The couple’s son was run over and killed by a train while he was heading for the beach festival in December 2025

title: Days after launching party, V. Pugazhendi pledges support to DMK
content: After Jayalalithaa’s demise, when the AIADMK faced a leadership crisis, Mr. Pugazhendi threw his weight behind Jayalalithaa’s aide Sasikala and her nephew T.T.V. Dhinakaran.

title: Kochi Corporation in Kerala faces huge biomedical waste backlog as incinerator capacity is reduced
content: One of two incinerators at Common Bio-Medical Waste Treatment Facility of KEIL remained shut for 15 days last month. Though the incinerator is back in operation, KEIL has restricted intake to three tonnes a day, down from five tonnes, as it prioritises hospital waste. One of two incinerators at Brahmapuram developed a snag during trial run

title: Economic Stabilisation Fund to help government respond to global challenges: Sitharaman
content: “The fiscal deficit for the current financial year (2025-26) will be within the Revised Estimates,” Ms. Sitharaman said

title: PM Modi virtually unveils projects worth ₹4,570 crore in Assam's Kokrajhar
content: PM Modi unveiled the projects through video conference from Guwahati after his Kokrajhar visit was cancelled due to heavy rain.

title: Rahul Gandhi spreading misinformation on energy issue, says Giriraj Singh
content: Rahul Gandhi said in the Lok Sabha that the war between the U.S. and Israel, and Iran is going to have far-reaching consequences

title: EAM Jaishankar, Iran’s Araghchi hold 4th phone call since start of West Asian conflict
content: The conversation comes as India ramped up efforts to secure safe passage to the Indian-flagged merchant vessels through the strategic shipping route of Strait of Hormuz that has been partially blocked by Tehran

title: Is an MLA contesting MP poll on another party’s B-Form not defection? asks Harish Rao
content: BRS leader says Khairatabad legislator Danam Nagender was elected as an MLA on the ticket of BRS and openly contested Lok Sabha elections using Congress B-Form

title: Ramzan 2026 : Meet a few chefs who prepare ‘nombu kanji’ at mosques in Thiruvananthapuram
content: Mosques hire proficient hands to prepare the spicy porridge in huge quantities, which devotees have while breaking their fast

title: Justice Lisa Gill assumes charge as AP High Court Judge
content: Justice Lisa Gill has been transferred to the Andhra Pradesh High Court from the Punjab and Haryana High Court, where she had been serving as a Judge

title: T.N. CM Stalin commissions first unit of Udangudi Thermal Power Plant, inaugurates various projects
content: Infrastructural projects, including thermal power plants, roads, schools, worth crores saw inauguration by CM Stalin across Tamil Nadu

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: Kongunadu Makkal Desiya Katchi to contest in two seats on DMK’s ‘Rising Sun’ symbol
content: The seat-sharing agreement was signed between DMK president and Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin and KMDK general secretary E.R. Eswaran at Anna Arivalayam, the DMK headquarters in Chennai

title: Curbs imposed in parts of Kashmir; protests break out in Shia dominated areas
content: The protesters shouted slogans against the U.S. and Israel aggression in Iran, and in support of Palestine

title: Naidu lauds farmer for impressive results through natural farming
content: Rosaiah has cultivated 52 varieties of crops on 75 cents of land by following natural farming practices

title: Cash-for-query case: Supreme Court stays Delhi HC order on sanction for CBI to try Mahua Moitra
content: The cash-for-query case pertains to the allegation that Mahua Moitra asked questions in the Lok Sabha in exchange for cash and gifts from a businessman

title: LPG crisis: Who gets priority in supply of cylinders in Karnataka — hospitals, hostels and residential schools, says Minister
content: Hotels will have to wait for at least a week by which time supply of LPG cylinders are expected to ease: Muniyappa

title: Rajya Sabha election: BJD, Congress ask MLAs not to leave Bhubaneswar till March 16
content: With five candidates in the fray for four Rajya Sabha vacancies, political analysts foresee the possibility of cross-voting, absenteeism, or even intentional rejection of votes

title: LPG shortage forces closure of over 100 Kochi hotels
content: Some eateries that remain open have pared down menus to avoid a complete shutdown, restricting certain items to specific time slots

title: Sasikala announces her party’s name: All India Puratchi Thalaivar Makkal Munnetra Kazhagam
content: Earlier on February 24, 2026 she unveiled the party’s flag at a public meeting in Kamuthi of Ramanathapuram district.

title: INDIA bloc MPs submit notice seeking removal of CEC Gyanesh Kumar in Parliament
content: 193 Parliamentarians, 130 from Lok Sabha and 63 from Rajya Sabha, sign 10-page notice listing seven charges against Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar

title: Mandatory booking interval for LPG cylinders is 45 days in rural areas, 25 days in urban areas: Telangana Govt
content: Commercial LPG supply has been temporarily restricted to prioritize essential sectors such as hospitals, educational institutions, old age homes, orphanages and government bodies

title: LPG issue: Lok Sabha proceedings adjourned repeatedly amid Opposition protests
content: Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju slammed Congress leader Rahul Gandhi for refusing to understand the pulse of the people

title: Karnataka’s ₹391 crore plan to dump pollution from Bengaluru on farmers in Kanakapura, get contaminated vegetables in return
content: The report by Mapping Malnad alleges that the government is trying to solve a problem by geographical displacement of pollution

title: DRI officials raid illegal Alprazolam manufacturing unit in A.P., seize drugs, worth ₹47 Crore
content: Under ‘Operation White Hammer’, the DRI officials conducted searches on a private chemicals manufacturing factory, which was manufacturing Alprazolam tablets, which comes under psychotropic substance

title: Paid menstrual pain leave may cost women their careers, Supreme Court apprehends
content: Chief Justice Kant distinguished between creating a legally enforceable statutory right and a spontaneous act or policy from employers towards their women employees

title: Watch: Women lead the way in this Tamil Nadu port terminal
content: As the world celebrates International Women’s Day on March 8, a port terminal in Tamil Nadu’s Thoothukudi district is quietly setting an inspiring example. Nearly 45% of the workforce at berth 9 of V.O. Chidambaranar Port are women , working across critical operational roles, including administrative roles, as rubber tyre gantry (RTG) crane operators, wharf checkers, medical personnel, and as members of the planning team.

title: Sun TV Network sues Chennai Super Kings for using Jailer, Jailer 2, and Coolie songs in jersey promo
content: Justice Senthilkumar Ramamoorthy adjourns the hearing of the copyright infringment suit to Monday for CSK Limited to file an affidavit that it shall not use the songs without authorisation

title: LPG crisis: Malayalam film industry starts feeling the heat as production schedules hit
content: With nearly 18 films in production, Kerala Film Producers’ Association warns of a potential ₹5 crore loss if the crisis continues

title: Section of Bengal govt employees on ‘cease-work’ over dearness allowance dues
content: The protest, called by the ‘Sangrami Joutha Mancha’ to press the State government for payment of DA arrears, and implementation of a recent Supreme Court direction on the issue

title: Supreme Court steps in to save the home of gharials at Chambal
content: The bench said that even areas where the Madhya Pradesh Chief Minister had released gharials have come under illegal mining

title: Technopark IT firms in Kerala mull shifting to WFH as LPG shortage hits food services
content: Crisis due to LPG shortage hits paying guest accommodations and hostels in Kazhakuttam area, where young IT professionals from other districts and States stay

title: Deploying specialist doctors in PHCs not illegal, rules Madras High Court
content: Justice D. Bharatha Chakravarthy, however, directs Tamil Nadu government to publish on its website the details regarding doctors on bond service and their places of posting

title: LPG cylinder stolen from restaurant in Kerala capital amid shortage
content: Hotel owner says accused entered the premises after breaking open the door of the kitchen area and took away a filled LPG cylinder

title: Madhya Pradesh: Official suspended after farmer’s video of crying in mandi to sell crop goes viral
content: The incident took place at the Nanakhedi Krishi Upaj Mandi in Guna when the purchasing was suspended allegedly due to a market inspector’s misbehaviour with the farmers and traders

title: LPG crisis: Shortage of commercial gas hits more sectors in Kerala’s Kozhikode district
content: Hoteliers, drivers of LPG-powered auto-rickshaws, charity organisations engaged in supply of free meals worst hit. Two gas crematoriums in Kozhikode city have stopped bookings

title: 86% of LPG supply in Telangana used domestically; problems in commercial supply come to our notice: Uttam
content: Telangana Civil Supplies Minister called for a meeting with the representatives of Oil Marketing Companies (OMC)  on Friday

title: Stronger air defence takeaway for India from West Asia conflict: Ex IAF chief
content: “Drones are going to play a “huge role” in any forthcoming future conflict that we can envisage,” the former IAF chief said

title: Rise in temperatures across Maharashtra, showers predicted post-March 14
content: Coastal regions, including Mumbai, Thane, Palghar, and the broader Konkan belt, are bracing for hot and humid weather

title: Six MPs urge Centre to include reservation Act of Karnataka in Ninth Schedule
content: The MPs expressed concern that the proposal sent by the government of Karnataka to include the Act in the Ninth Schedule has been pending with the Union government for more than three years

title: GMIT to hold 21st convocation in Davanagere on March 14
content: A total of 708 students including 649 graduates (B.E.) students and 59 postgraduates (MBA) students will receive their degrees during the 21st convocation of GM Institute of Technology (GMIT) in Davanagere on March 14

title: On the streets for survival
content: Economic hardship, social customs, displacement, and sudden life setbacks continue to push people into begging even as rehabilitation efforts struggle to keep pace. Nellore Sravani reports from Vijayawada on the lives caught between survival and law

title: NITI Aayog praises Kalaburagi district in Karnataka for achieving 100% institutional deliveries
content: Earlier in November 2025, Kalaburagi district’s administration had been appreciated by NITI Aayog for implementing innovative governance and development initiatives to ensure sustained progress in its aspirational blocks

title: All crest gates of Tungabhadra dam to be replaced by May: Karnataka Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar
content: As of March 10, the installation of 14 crest gates had been completed. Work on three more gates was in progress. According to technical experts, installation of one crest gate normally takes about seven days.

title: Neighbourhood clinics on crutches
content: Across Hyderabad’s crowded localities, Urban Primary Health Centres serve as the first point of contact for thousands seeking everyday medical care. Often housed in ageing buildings and run by small teams of doctors, nurses and community health workers, these clinics shoulder much of the city’s routine healthcare burden. But while the city gains prominence for its corporate hospitals and medical tourism, UPHCs continue to function with limited infrastructure and growing patient loads, writes Siddharth Kumar Singh

title: Kalaburagi-Bengaluru Vande Bharat Express train completes two years of service, records occupancy rate of 103.52% in 2026
content: The train covers a distance of 561 km in about eight hours, compared with 10 to 12 hours by other superfast trains, with halts at Yadgir, Raichur, Manthralayam Road, Guntakal, Anantapur, Sai P. Nilayam and Yelahanka

title: Former West Bengal Governor Ananda Bose buries hatchet with Educationists’ Forum head Omprakash Mishra
content: Professor Omprakash Mishra called former Governor an erudite person and said that he was polite and generous with his appreciation during the meeting

title: Rajisha Vijayan: I was flabbergasted when Krishand first told me the story of ‘Masthishka Maranam’
content: Rajisha Vijayan talks about how she transformed to become Frida Soman in the sci-fi satire Masthishka Maranam - A Frankenbiting of Simon’s Memories, directed by Krishand

title: LPG supply fears spark induction stove rush in India
content: West Asia tensions disrupt LPG supply chains, pushing households and restaurants toward electric cooking alternatives

title: Four killed as bus overturns on NH-44 in Nizamabad district
content: The deceased were identified as Manjusha, 30, her seven-year-old son Rishi. While the body of Abbas, 40, has been identified, the identity of another male victim is yet to be established

title: The Supreme Court judgement on creamy layer exclusion for OBC quota | Explained
content: The judgement came on March 11 in a case that was meant to clear decades-long confusion about how to apply the crucial income/wealth test prescribed in regulations, specifically for OBC candidates whose parents are officials of Central or State PSUs or in private employment

title: Experts clash over HALEU-Th fuel for Indian nuclear reactors
content: Indian scientists and international experts are divided over a study questioning the viability of a nuclear reactor fuel called HALEU-thorium; while critics cite safety risks, proponents argue the technology is vital to achieve national energy goals

title: 29 new bridges proposed on Musi river in Hyderabad
content: The Comprehensive Mobility Plan under preparation as precursor to the master plan of Hyderabad Metropolitan Region for 2050, has proposed 29 bridges on the Musi river

title: India-made app turns impaired speech into clear speech in near-realtime
content: The delay is only a few hundred milliseconds, and the developers are now focusing on regional languages, project leader Vineet Gandhi says

title: Women’s political participation in India
content: While the turnout of women voters has increased over the years, campaign participation still shows a clear gender gap. The disparity in Parliamentary representation also remains wide, reflecting limited candidature, party nominations, and constraints on political autonomy.

title: CII-Rajasthan annual session held with call to promote entrepreneurship among youth
content: The Chief Minister said the State government had introduced as many as 34 new policies to create a conducive environment for industries

title: Cost escalation and delays haunt Bengaluru Metro project
content: Bengaluru has India’s second largest metro network, with around 96 kilometres of operational track and 83 stations. Darshan Devaiah B.P. argues that this key transport project, built with ₹67,460 crore so far, needs to keep deadlines to be cost effective and serve the public better

title: Rumours being spread about LPG shortage, says Punjab CM Mann
content: Cautioning against attempts to exploit the situation, the Chief Minister said the government will act firmly against anyone trying to create panic or manipulate the supply of essential goods

title: WB BJP leaders meet PM ahead of mega Brigade Rally in Kolkata
content: The meeting comes 48 hours before the Prime Minister is scheduled to address a mega rally at Kolkata’s Brigade Parade Grounds on Saturday. According to sources in the party, the first list of BJP candidates contesting Assembly polls is likely to be announced in the next few days

title: Akhilesh Yadav meets Avimukteshwaranand Saraswati, vows to put an end to ‘fake saints’
content: Samajwadi Party chief said the responsibility of the LPG crisis in India is on the BJP government, both at the Centre and State in U.P.

title: Goa, desired and disputed: Review of Appetite, an anthology of stories, essays and poems
content: The collection attempts to look beyond clichés, to provoke debates around belonging, settlement and identity

title: In Bihar, companies surrendering sand mining lease will not be allowed to participate in fresh tender
content: 78 companies surrendered sand mining leases citing massive losses. The Bihar minister said the move has caused a revenue loss to the tune of around ₹700 crore to the department

title: VBSA Bill a ‘solution’ to current challenges, ‘catalyst’ for growth: UGC tells joint panel of Parliament
content: Education Ministry had told the joint panel that the NEP 2020 was prepared after one of the widest consultation processes; the Bill was circulated amongst 39 Central Ministries and Departments for consultations

title: CSR Initiative to provide mental health education to 10,000 students in Karnataka and Tamil Nadu
content: These activity-based sessions are designed to make conversations around emotions, empathy, communication, and well-being accessible to young people and educators alike

title: Missiles and drones strike at the heart of a village in Odisha
content: The war in West Asia has thrown the world into chaos. Its impact is even being felt in a remote village in Odisha, where almost every family has a male member working in the Gulf. Satyasundar Barik speaks to residents of Manikapur in Ganjam district about how they are coping with the anxiety

title: Kochi Corporation weighs fresh options as KSINC faces criticism over ro-ro service
content: Move comes after councillors across party lines voiced sharp criticism of KSINC over mounting losses and urged the civic body to identify a new operator to ease its financial burden

title: LPG crisis: PM Modi hits out at those creating panic; asks States to monitor black marketers, hoarders
content: The prime minister also said the government is actively working on multiple fronts, and in the past few days, he spoke with the leaders of several countries about the developments in West Asia

title: Kerala nurses on the warpath over long-overdue wage revision
